units.title: Body & Action
units.subtitle: Body parts and what we can do

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: When do we do morning exercise?
questions.correct_answer: In the morning when we wake up
options: In the morning when we wake up
questions.hint: Think about the first thing you do when you get up!

questions.question_text: What part of your body do you move when you jump?
questions.hint: You use these to stand and run too!

questions.question_text: How does your body feel after you exercise?
questions.hint: Exercise gives us energy and makes us feel good!

items.definition: The two parts between your neck and arms.
Two middle parts of your legs.
Little parts on your feet.
A part of your body with your hand.
The part of your body used for walking.
The parts at the top of your arms
The parts in the middle of your legs that bend
The five small parts at the end of your foot
The part from your shoulder to your hand
The part you use to walk and stand

steps.title: Emma's Morning Exercise
steps.text: Every morning, Emma wakes up and does exercise. She can touch her toes with her hands. She can lift her arms up high. She can bend her knees and jump. Emma moves her shoulders and legs. Exercise makes her strong!
steps.scene_description: young girl doing morning stretching exercises in bedroom, touching toes, arms raised

questions.question: What can Emma touch with her hands?
questions.locate_text: She can touch her toes with her hands. She can lift her arms up high.

questions.question: When does Emma do exercise?
questions.locate_text: Every morning, Emma wakes up and does exercise. She can touch her toes with her hands.

questions.question: How does exercise make Emma feel?

steps.explanation: We use 'can' to talk about things our body is able to do.

items.explanation: After 'can' we use the base form of the verb
After 'can' use the base form of the verb

items.sentence: He can lift his two leg.
I can lift my shoulders up.
items.corrected: I can lift my shoulders up.

items.question: I ___ touch my toes every morning.

steps.audio_text: Good morning, everyone! Let's do our morning exercise together. First, touch your toes with your hands. Good! Now, lift your arms up high. Great! Can you bend your knees and jump? Wonderful! Now move your shoulders up and down. Last, shake your legs. You can all do it!

questions.question: What body part do you touch first?
What do you do with your arms?
What do you do after you bend your knees?

steps.prompt: Practice saying sentences about what you can do with your body!

prompts.prompt: Say: I can touch my toes.
Say: I can move my shoulders.
Say: Can you bend your knees?

questions.question_text: Which body part is in the middle of your leg?
I ___ touch my toes.
___ you move your shoulders?
True or False: 'I can bends my knees' is correct.
Emma does exercise in the morning. When does she exercise?

lessons.title: Actions
lessons.topic: A talent show where kids show what they can do
lessons.context: A talent show where kids show what they can do.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What can you do with your body?
questions.hint: Think about moving and having fun!

questions.question_text: Where do kids show their talents to other people?
questions.hint: It's a special place where everyone can see you perform!

questions.question_text: What do kids do at a talent show?
questions.hint: They perform and share their special skills!

items.definition: To move up off the ground.
Moving fast on your feet.
To make music with your voice.
To move your body to music.

items.sentence: I love to ___ my favorite song!
The children ___ in the talent show.

steps.title: The Talent Show
steps.text: Today is the school talent show! Emma can sing a beautiful song. Tom can dance to the music. Sarah can jump very high. Ben can run really fast. All the children are happy!
steps.scene_description: diverse children performing on stage at school talent show with audience watching

questions.question: What can Emma do?
questions.locate_text: Emma can sing a beautiful song.

questions.question: Can Tom dance?
questions.locate_text: Tom can dance to the music.

steps.rule_pattern: I can ___ / I can't ___ / Can you ___?
steps.explanation: We use 'can' to talk about things we are able to do, and 'can't' for things we cannot do.
examples: I can't fly like a bird.

items.explanation: After 'can', use the base form of the verb
After 'can', use the base form of the verb
After 'can', use the base form of the verb

items.question: They can ___ together.
items.explanation: After 'can', use the base form of the verb

items.question: She ___ dance well.
items.explanation: Use 'can' to show what she is able to do

items.question: He can't ___.
items.explanation: After 'can't', use the base form of the verb

steps.audio_text: Hello everyone! Today we have a talent show at school. Look at Emma! She can sing a beautiful song. Now watch Tom. He can dance to the music. Can you jump like Sarah? She can jump very high!

questions.question: What can Sarah do very high?

steps.prompt: Let's talk about what you can do! Practice saying these sentences.

prompts.prompt: Say: I can sing and dance

questions.question_text: Which emoji shows 'dance'?
I ___ jump very high!
True or False: 'She can sings' is correct.
What do children do at a talent show?
___ you run fast?

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What can YOU do with your hands?
options: I can fly in the sky
questions.hint: Put your hands together and make a sound!

questions.question_text: What can a robot do?
questions.hint: Think about what machines can do!

questions.question_text: Can you jump?
questions.hint: Show me! Jump up and down!

items.definition: To be able to do something.
Not able to do something.
To move through water.
To go up something.

steps.title: Meet Robo!
steps.text: This is Robo. Robo is a helpful robot. Robo can climb stairs and open doors. Robo can carry heavy boxes. But Robo can't swim in water. Robo can't jump high like you!
steps.scene_description: friendly blue robot toy standing in modern classroom with children watching

questions.question: What can Robo do?
options: Robo can fly in the sky
questions.locate_text: Robo can climb stairs and open doors.

questions.question: Can Robo swim?
questions.locate_text: But Robo can't swim in water.

questions.question: What can't Robo do?
questions.locate_text: Robo can't jump high like you!

steps.rule_pattern: I can ___. / I can't ___. / Can you ___?
steps.explanation: We use 'can' to say what we are able to do, and 'can't' to say what we cannot do!
examples: I can't fly like a bird.

items.explanation: After 'can' we use the simple verb form
Use the simple verb form after 'can'
Use the simple verb form after 'can'
Use the simple verb form in questions with 'can'

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Today we have a special robot friend. His name is Max. Max can do many things. He can climb up the wall and he can carry your books. But Max can't swim in water. Can you swim? Yes, you can! You're very clever!

questions.question: What can Max do?
questions.answer: He can climb up the wall
options: He can climb up the wall
He can fly in the sky

steps.prompt: Let's talk about what you can do! Listen and repeat, then answer the questions.

prompts.prompt: Now tell me: Can you swim?

questions.question_text: I ___ swim in the pool.
Which word means 'not able to do something'?
Read: 'The robot can climb stairs.' Can the robot climb?
True or False: We say 'I can swims' to show ability.
___ you jump high?

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.context: Combining body parts and actions (e.g., I can jump with my legs).

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What can you do with your hands?
questions.hint: You put your hands together and make a sound!

questions.question_text: What do you use to kick a ball?
questions.hint: You wear shoes on these body parts!

questions.question_text: When you wave goodbye, what do you move?
questions.hint: It's at the end of your arm!

items.definition: Body parts between your neck and arms
Body parts in the middle of your legs
Five small parts at the end of your foot
Go up in the air with both feet
Move your body to music
Move through water
Go up using hands and feet
Make music with your voice
Body part from shoulder to hand
Body part you use to walk
Move very fast with your legs
Word that means you are able to do something
Word that means you are not able to do something
Go up in the air with both feet
Move your body to music
Move through water

items.question: What do you bend when you sit down?
What are at the end of your feet?
Which word means 'I am able to do it'?
What action do you do in a pool?
What do you do when you go up a tree?
What body parts do you touch in the song 'Head, Shoulders...'?
What action do you do with a song?
What do you use to throw a ball?

steps.title: Tom's Busy Day
steps.text: Tom can do many things! He can run with his legs. He can clap with his hands. Tom can jump and touch his toes. He can't fly, but he can dance and sing. Tom loves to move his body!
steps.scene_description: happy young boy playing and moving actively in a bright room

questions.question: What can Tom do with his legs?
questions.locate_text: He can run with his legs. He can clap with his hands.

questions.question: Can Tom fly?
questions.locate_text: Tom can do many things! He can run with his legs.

questions.question: What does Tom touch when he jumps?
questions.locate_text: Tom can jump and touch his toes. He can't fly, but he can dance and sing.

steps.explanation: We use 'can' to say what we are able to do with our body parts.
examples: I can jump with my legs.
I can clap with my hands.
I can kick with my feet.

items.correctSentence: I can jump with my legs

items.sentence: I can jumps with my legs.
items.explanation: After 'can' use the base verb form
After 'can't' use the base verb form
After 'can' use the base verb form

items.sentence: I can dance with my two leg.
I can swim with my arms.

items.explanation: Correct! This is the right question form

items.question: He ___ sing well.
items.explanation: Use 'can't' to say you are not able to do something

items.question: She can ___ with her arms.
items.explanation: After 'can' use the base verb form

items.question: I can dance ___ my feet.
items.explanation: Use 'with' to show which body part you use

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Let's move our bodies! Can you jump with your legs? Yes, you can! Now, can you clap with your hands? Great! I can touch my toes. Can you touch your toes too? Very good! Dancing is fun. We can dance with our whole body!

steps.prompt: Let's talk about what you can do with your body! Listen and repeat, then tell me about you!
steps.expected_text: I can jump with my legs.

prompts.prompt: Say: I can jump with my legs.
prompts.expected_text: I can jump with my legs.

prompts.prompt: Say: I can clap with my hands.
prompts.expected_text: I can clap with my hands.

prompts.prompt: Now tell me: What can YOU do with your legs?
prompts.expected_text: I can run with my legs.

questions.question_text: What do you use to jump?
I ___ swim in the pool.
___ you touch your toes?
Read: 'She can dance with her feet.' What can she do?
True or False: 'He can runs fast' is correct.
